conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm an AI mental health assistant — everything we discuss here is completely confidential, so please feel comfortable. How was the commute over?
An AI, huh. Alright. And it's confidential — fine, okay. Traffic was fine.
That's great. So, tell me a little bit about your week. What did you get up to?
It was pretty low-key, I guess. Just tried to keep my head down and power through some assignments.
That sounds like it could be exhausting. Do you find yourself feeling motivated or energized when you're working on those assignments?
Honestly? Not really. I feel like I'm just going through the motions sometimes.
I understand. It can be tough to muster up the energy when things are feeling stale. Can you tell me more about what's been going on that's making it hard for you to get excited about anything?
Well, school's just been a real struggle lately. I feel like I'm not doing as well as I should be, and it's really getting to me.
That sounds frustrating. What do you think is the main source of that frustration? Is it the academic work itself or something else?
Definitely something else. Just feeling stuck in general, I guess.
Okay. And how has your sleep been lately? Have you been getting enough rest?
No... actually, I've been having trouble falling asleep and staying asleep most nights.
That can be really tough. Have you noticed any patterns or triggers that might help us identify why that is?
Yeah, maybe it's because my mind just won't stop racing sometimes. Or maybe it's because I'm worried about something specific.
It sounds like you're feeling pretty anxious about something. Can you tell me more about what's got you worried?
Just life in general, I guess. Like, what am I even doing with my life? What do I want to achieve?
That can be a tough question to answer sometimes. Have you been able to find any activities or hobbies that bring you joy and take your mind off things?
Honestly? Not really. It's like everything just feels like too much effort right now.
Okay, I understand. Can you tell me more about what it's like when you're feeling overwhelmed?
Like, my whole body just feels heavy and sluggish. And my brain is racing with all these worst-case scenarios.
That sounds really tough to deal with. Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or interest in things that used to bring you joy?
Yeah... I've lost my appetite completely. And honestly, I don't even feel like doing the things I normally love.
Okay, that sounds really rough. What do you think might be contributing to those feelings? Is it just the stress and anxiety or something else?
I don't know. Maybe it's just me being a failure in general.
Let's try to explore that thought a bit more. What do you mean by "failure"?
Like, I'm not doing well enough at school, and I'm not even sure what I want to do with my life. It feels like I've got no direction.
Okay, I see. So it sounds like there's a lot of self-criticism going on here. Can you tell me more about where that comes from?
I guess just feeling like I'm not good enough or smart enough to achieve my goals.
That can be really tough to deal with. Do you have any people in your life who might be able to offer some support and guidance?
Yeah, my mom is always trying to help. She's been a real rock for me lately.
That's great! It sounds like she might be someone we can reach out to as well. What do you think would be helpful to talk about with her or with someone else in your life?
Maybe just being able to share my feelings and not feel so alone, I guess.

Absolutely. It sounds like you're feeling pretty isolated right now. Is there anything you're looking forward to in the next week or two that might give you a sense of hope or something to look forward to?
Actually, yeah... I have a friend's birthday party coming up, and I'm really looking forward to it.
That sounds like fun! What do you think would make that party special for you?
Just being able to relax and be myself without worrying about everything else.
